data.title: Planet Labs Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.02 beats by $0.02, revenue of $61.3M misses by $1.82M
data.description:
• Planet Labs press release (NYSE:PL): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.02 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $61.3M (+10.7% Y/Y) misses by $1.82M.
  • Ended the quarter with $242 million in cash, cash equivalents and short-term investments.

  • For the fourth quarter of fiscal year 2025, ending January 31, 2025, Planet expects revenue to be in the range of approximately $61 million to $63 million. Non-GAAP Gross Margin is expected to be in the range of approximately 63% to 65%. Adjusted EBITDA is expected to be in the range of approximately $0 to $2 million for the quarter. Capital Expenditures are expected to be in the range of approximately $8 million and $11 million for the quarter.

data.title: Dow ends lower, S&P 500, Nasdaq pull back from record highs as Nvidia loses ground
data.description:
Major U.S. stock averages fell Monday, with market behemoth Nvidia (NVDA) emerging as a source of downward pressure after large-cap stocks marked record highs and gains for the previous week.

The S&P 500 (SP500) -0.5%, only its second loss since December trade started. The Dow (DJI) -0.5%, and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) -0.6%. The benchmark (SP500) and the Nasdaq (COMP:IND) logged record-closing peaks on Friday, aided by accelerating expectations for another rate cut by the Federal Reserve. The Dow last week marked its first-ever close above 45,000.

This week, investors are keeping in sight consumer and wholesale inflation reports. The November consumer price index will land on Wednesday, and the producer price index will arrive Thursday. In the fixed-income market, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 3 basis points to 4.20%, and the 30-year yield (US30Y) picked up 4 basis points to 4.39%.

"With long-duration treasury yields firming up, broad U.S. equity indices registered a somewhat rare red day," Ahan Vashi, investing group leader of The Quantamental Investor, told Seeking Alpha. "Heading into year-end, animal spirits are running wild on Wall Street, and given seasonality trends, a Santa Rally cannot be ruled out," he said. "However, prudent investors must continue to manage risk proactively due to a highly uncertain macroeconomic and geopolitical backdrop."

Investors came into Monday's session seeing a fresh Street-high 2025 target for the S&P 500 (SP500), with Oppenheimer initiating its view of 7,100. But on its way to locking in double-digit gains for 2024, nine of the S&P 500's (SP500) 11 sectors fell Monday. Financials paced decliners by losing -1.4%, and Consumer Discretionary, last week's top-performing sector, slipped. Health Care and Real Estate rose.

AI chipmaker Nvidia (NVDA), part of all three key indexes, was a notable decliner Monday, down -2.6%, but ending off session lows. China's antitrust agency reportedly opened an investigation into the second most-valuable publicly traded company on concerns it may have breached monopoly laws. The news was first reported domestically by Bloomberg.

Nvidia (NVDA) said in a statement that it "wins on merit," and that it was "happy to answer any questions regulators may have" about its business.

Meanwhile, separate deal-related developments for Interpublic Group of Companies (IPG) and Hershey (HSY) propelled their shares on the S&P 500 (SP500). Hershey (HSY) +10.9%, topping the index after Bloomberg reported that Mondelez (MDLZ) is evaluating a potential takeover of the chocolate maker. Interpublic (IPG) +3.6% after the company agreed to be purchased by rival advertising agency Omnicom (NYSE:OMC). Mondelez (MDLZ) fell -2.3%, and Omnicom tumbled -10.3%.

Among other individual movers, Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) -5.5% after Bank of America cut its rating on the semiconductor company to Neutral from Buy, on AI- and PC-related competition concerns.

Workday (WDAY) +5.1% while Apollo Global Management (APO) -3% after S&P Dow Jones Indices said Friday the companies will be added to the S&P 500 (SP500). The moves will take place before trading opens Monday, December 23.

data.title: Twilio snapped six straight sessions of gains
data.description:
Twilio (NYSE:TWLO) shares snapped six straight sessions of gains, as the stock ended 2.8% lower at $110.21 on Monday.

The information technology stock advanced 8.5% in the preceding six sessions. The stock has surged 49.5% so far this year, above the 27.7% rise in the broader S&P 500 Index.

TWLO is up 19% over the past one month.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, TWLO has a Strong Buy rating with a score of 4.95 out of 5. The company received B in the prospect of profitability, A in momentum, while it got A- in growth factor.

Turning to the Wall Street community, 15 analysts gave TWLO a Buy and above. 12 analysts have given the stock a Hold recommendation, while three recommended Sell or lower.

Seeking Alpha analysts are also positive and see the stock as a Buy.

Earlier in November, Wells Fargo upgraded Twilio on its new build cycle. The analysts noted that they believe Twilio can serve as a pick-and-shovel play for the next wave of AI-native front office and communications-powered generative AI applications.

“The company's growth revitalization plan, driven by AI-enhanced product features and cost-cutting measures, is proving effective,” pointed out a recent Seeking Alpha analysis.

data.title: McDonald's partners with Uber Eats in Canada
data.description:
McDonald's (NYSE:MCD) and Uber Technologies (NYSE:UBER) inked a deal that will see the restaurant company's Canadian business and Uber Eats give Uber One members access to exclusive savings on their favorite McDonald's menu items.

With Uber One, Canadians can now enjoy $0 delivery fees and 5% off eligible McDonald's orders from more than 1,300 locations across Canada.

"Our expanded partnership with McDonald's is a double win for Uber One members and McDonald's lovers alike," noted Lola Kassim, General Manager of Uber Eats in Canada.

McDonald's Canada, established in 1967, has grown to over 1,400 locations nationwide, serving around 2.5 million customers daily. The brand emphasizes a community-based strategy, tailoring its menu to local tastes with items like the Butter Chicken McBurger and Poutine.

data.title: Microsoft continues gains for seven straight sessions
data.description:
Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) continued gains for seven straight sessions as the stock closed 0.55% higher, at $446.02 on Monday.

The tech giant gained 4.75% in the last six trading sessions. The stock has gained more than 17% so far this year, compared to an over 27% rise in the broader S&P 500 Index.

MSFT is up 5% over the past one month. The stock closed 0.21% higher, at $443.57 on Friday.

According to an Axios report from over the weekend, the U.S. government has approved the export of advanced AI chips to a Microsoft-operated facility in the United Arab Emirates as part of the company's collaboration with G42.

Microsoft’s $1.5B investment in the Abu Dhabi-based AI firm was reported in April. The deal would give the U.S. tech giant a minority stake and a board seat. However, the deal was scrutinized after U.S. lawmakers raised concerns that G42 could potentially transfer U.S. AI technology to China.

Seeking Alpa’s Quant rating has rated the Microsoft (MSFT) stock a HOLD, with a score of 3.43 out of 5. SA authors are also cautious and have rated the stock a HOLD.

The sell-side analysts, on the other hand, see Microsoft (MSFT) stock as a STRONG BUY, with 50 out of 55 analysts recommending Buy and above.

Seeking Alpha analyst Kenio Fontes believes Microsoft's essential products and consistent revenue growth justify its premium valuation.

“Current valuation reflects expected growth, but potential positive surprises in cloud revenue and AI integration could enhance returns,” Fontes added.

data.title: Shell, Hess tapped as top big energy stocks at Wells Fargo
data.description:
Shell (NYSE:SHEL) +2.4% and Hess (NYSE:HES) +1.1% in Monday's trading as Wells Fargo names the companies as its top picks within the integrated oil and international E&P coverage, respectively, expecting the oil and gas sector will deliver positive returns in 2025 through spending discipline, even with a lackluster oil price forecast.

Despite the muted oil price outlook, Wells Fargo analysts led by Roger Read believe Shell (SHEL) could still generate substantial free cash flow relative to its CFFO generation through 2026 (65%-70% conversion).

Shell (SHEL) did narrow the valuation discount to its U.S. peers during past two years, as coming in light on capital spending and heavy on CFFO is typically a solid recipe for earning investors' appreciation, Read writes, adding that if the company can continue to execute on its operational and financial performance, the valuation discount to its U.S. peers can continue to narrow.

Read's positive view of Hess (HES) remains underpinned by the company's position in the very remarkable exploration results and ongoing developments in Guyana, a region "without peer within the industry in terms of resource, cost to develop, benign environment and stable political backdrop."

If commodity prices turn out stronger than expected, Hess' (HES) cash returns to shareholders could exceed its peers in 2025 and beyond, the analyst says.

More broadly, most IOCs and international E&Ps have established stated cash return goals for 2025, and Read and his team anticipate cash payouts of more than 30% as common across the sector next year.

Wells Fargo also maintains Overweight ratings on Chevron (CVX), ConocoPhillips (COP), Exxon Mobil (XOM) and Suncor Energy (SU).

data.title: Vertex upgraded to buy by Jefferies on fundamentals, pipeline
data.description:
• Jefferies has upgraded Vertex Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:VRTX) to buy from hold, citing improving fundamentals and the company’s late-stage pipeline drugs for pain and APOL1-mediated kidney disease.
  • The investment bank said it likes the recent pullback in the stock and sees “$5-7B+ of pipeline coming in the next leg of growth in 2026-2030.”
  • “VRTX (VRTX) is a clean growth name and should continue to be owned and favored by generalists and long onlies based on solid fundamentals and a growing pipeline, and as part of a scarce growth asset within large biopharma with no major data risk in 2025, no obesity volatility or exposure, etc.,” said Jefferies in a note.
  • Jefferies raised its price target for the stock to $550 from $500.

data.title: HIG Europe submits offer for Kantar Media -Sky News
data.description:
• Private equity firm HIG Europe submitted a formal bid for Kantar Media, which is reportedly valued at about £1b.
  • There's no certainty that a deal will happen, according to a Sky News item on Monday.
  • Kantar Media measures ratings data in many countries around the world. Kantar Media is a unit of Kantar Group, which is majority-owned by PE firm Bain Capital. WPP Plc (NYSE:WPP) has a minority stake in Kantar Media.
  • Last week, France's Ipsos (OTCPK:IPSOF) was considering a bid for Kantar Media, thought it dropped out of the process, according to the report.
